passage_de: Der Flug von Sizilien nach Frankfurt dauert zwei Stunden. Das Flugzeug ist groß und modern. Ich trinke einen Kaffee und lese eine Zeitung. Die Landung ist pünktlich.
passage_en: The flight from Sicily to Frankfurt takes two hours. The plane is big and modern. I drink a coffee and read a newspaper. The landing is on time.

questions.question_en: How long does the flight take?
questions.model_answer_en: The flight takes two hours.

questions.question_en: What does the writer do on the plane?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer drinks a coffee and reads a newspaper.

questions.question_en: What is the landing like?
questions.model_answer_en: The landing is on time.

passage_de: Ich stehe am Gepäckband, aber mein Koffer kommt nicht. Alle anderen Leute nehmen ihre Taschen und gehen. Mein Gepäck ist weg. Das ist eine Katastrophe!
passage_en: I am standing at the baggage belt, but my suitcase does not arrive. All the other people take their bags and leave. My luggage is gone. This is a catastrophe!

questions.question_en: What problem does the writer have?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer’s suitcase does not arrive and the luggage is missing.

questions.question_en: What do the other people do?
questions.model_answer_en: They take their bags and leave.

questions.question_en: How does the writer feel about the situation?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer thinks it is a catastrophe.

passage_de: Ich gehe zum Schalter für verlorenes Gepäck. Der Beamte fragt: 'Wie sieht Ihr Koffer aus?' Ich sage: 'Er ist blau und ziemlich groß.' Er schreibt alles auf.
passage_en: I go to the lost luggage counter. The official asks, 'What does your suitcase look like?' I say, 'It is blue and quite big.' He writes everything down.

questions.question_en: Where does the writer go?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer goes to the lost luggage counter.

questions.question_en: How is the suitcase described?
questions.model_answer_en: It is blue and quite big.

questions.question_en: What does the official do after hearing the description?
questions.model_answer_en: He writes everything down.

passage_de: Frankfurt ist eine interessante Stadt. Es gibt viele Wolkenkratzer und alte Häuser. Das Wetter ist heute schön und die Sonne scheint. Ich finde die Stadt toll.
passage_en: Frankfurt is an interesting city. There are many skyscrapers and old houses. The weather is nice today and the sun is shining. I think the city is great.

questions.question_en: What kinds of buildings are mentioned in Frankfurt?
questions.model_answer_en: There are many skyscrapers and old houses.

questions.question_en: What is the weather like?
questions.model_answer_en: The weather is nice and the sun is shining.

questions.question_en: What does the writer think of the city?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer thinks the city is great.

passage_de: Die Zeil ist eine berühmte Einkaufsstraße. Man kann dort alles kaufen: Kleidung, Schuhe und Handys. Ich brauche eine neue Zahnbürste und ein paar Socken.
passage_en: The Zeil is a famous shopping street. You can buy everything there: clothes, shoes and mobile phones. I need a new toothbrush and a pair of socks.

questions.question_en: What kind of place is the Zeil?
questions.model_answer_en: It is a famous shopping street.

questions.question_en: Name two things people can buy there.
questions.model_answer_en: People can buy clothes, shoes or mobile phones there.

questions.question_en: What does the writer need?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer needs a new toothbrush and a pair of socks.

passage_de: In der Zwischenzeit trage ich die Kleidung meines Bruders. Er ist ein bisschen größer als ich. Die Hose ist zu lang und das Hemd ist zu weit. Das ist lustig!
passage_en: In the meantime I wear my brother’s clothes. He is a little bigger than I am. The trousers are too long and the shirt is too wide. It is funny!

questions.question_en: Whose clothes is the writer wearing?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer is wearing the brother’s clothes.

questions.question_en: Why do the clothes not fit well?
questions.model_answer_en: They do not fit well because the brother is a bit bigger.

questions.question_en: What is wrong with the trousers and the shirt?
questions.model_answer_en: The trousers are too long and the shirt is too wide.

passage_de: Ich fahre mit der U-Bahn in die Innenstadt. Das Ticket ist nicht teuer. In der U-Bahn sehe ich viele Menschen aus verschiedenen Ländern. Die Fahrt dauert zehn Minuten.
passage_en: I travel by underground to the city centre. The ticket is not expensive. On the underground I see many people from different countries. The journey takes ten minutes.

questions.question_en: How does the writer travel to the city centre?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer travels by underground.

questions.question_en: What does the writer notice on the underground?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer sees many people from different countries.

questions.question_en: How long does the journey take?
questions.model_answer_en: The journey takes ten minutes.

passage_de: Ich besuche eine gemütliche Kneipe. Ich trinke einen Apfelwein. Das ist typisch für Frankfurt. Zum Essen gibt es Brot und Käse. Es schmeckt sehr gut.
passage_en: I visit a cosy pub. I drink an apple wine. That is typical for Frankfurt. To eat there is bread and cheese. It tastes very good.

questions.question_en: What does the writer drink?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer drinks an apple wine.

questions.question_en: What food is mentioned?
questions.model_answer_en: Bread and cheese are mentioned.

questions.question_en: What does the writer think of the food and drink?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer thinks it tastes very good.

passage_de: Ich besuche einen alten Freund in Frankfurt. Wir gehen zusammen am Main spazieren. Das Wasser ist ruhig und wir sehen viele Schiffe. Wir sprechen über alte Zeiten.
passage_en: I visit an old friend in Frankfurt. We go for a walk together along the Main. The water is calm and we see many ships. We talk about old times.

questions.question_en: Who does the writer visit?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer visits an old friend.

questions.question_en: Where do they go for a walk?
questions.model_answer_en: They go for a walk along the Main.

questions.question_en: What do they talk about?
questions.model_answer_en: They talk about old times.

passage_de: Am Abend bin ich müde. Ich gehe zurück in mein Zimmer. Ich hoffe, dass mein Koffer morgen ankommt. Ich gehe schlafen und träume von Frankfurt.
passage_en: In the evening I am tired. I go back to my room. I hope that my suitcase arrives tomorrow. I go to sleep and dream of Frankfurt.

questions.question_en: How does the writer feel in the evening?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer feels tired.

questions.question_en: What does the writer hope for?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer hopes that the suitcase will arrive tomorrow.

questions.question_en: What happens at the end of the passage?
questions.model_answer_en: The writer goes to sleep and dreams of Frankfurt.
